comment: Phase 7 Plan 02 — AnalogJS (@analogjs/vite-plugin-angular) defaults to resolving a sibling `tsconfig.app.json` for its AOT TS Program (the D-70 disk-cache path walks the Program's `include` patterns). Without it analogjs logs 'Unable to resolve tsconfig'. The visual-regression host imports the canonical `.rozie` files from `../../examples/`; @rozie/unplugin's Angular branch writes the `.rozie.ts` disk-cache artifacts alongside them, so the Program must include that directory.
pathsComment: The `baseUrl` + `paths` block exists because `examples/` is a SIBLING tree of `tests/visual-regression/`, and pnpm hoists @angular/* into vr's `node_modules/` only. TypeScript's default node-style module resolution from `examples/Card.rozie.ts` walks UP (`examples/node_modules`, `<repo>/node_modules`) and never reaches vr's tree, so `import { Component } from '@angular/core'` is unresolved inside @analogjs/vite-plugin-angular's NgtscProgram. NgtscProgram can still recognize the @Component decorator by AST scan, BUT when an `imports: [NgTemplateOutlet, FormsModule, ...]` array is present it must type-check every entry against the standalone-component metadata shape; unresolved symbols collapse the array to `any[]` and the analyzer SKIPS emitting `ɵcmp` for that class. The file falls through to esbuild's TS legacy-decorator transform, producing `__decorate(...)` runtime calls instead of static `ɵcmp`. At runtime Angular sees `@Component(...)` with no `ɵcmp` and falls back to JIT — but `@angular/compiler` is not in the browser bundle, so it throws `JIT compiler unavailable`. The `paths` block makes every Angular peer resolve regardless of importer location, so all `.rozie.ts` files (including those under `examples/`) get full AOT treatment.
